sectionDescription: Who we are, how the service works, and what makes TeleDirectMD different from large call-center style telemedicine.

items.question: What is TeleDirectMD?
items.answer: TeleDirectMD is a physician-led telehealth practice that provides $49 flat-fee, same-day video visits. The goal is to combine the convenience of telemedicine with the continuity and expertise of seeing a real physician. Adults 18 and older only. MD-only care. No insurance needed.

items.question: Who will I see during my visit?
items.answer: Every TeleDirectMD visit is with the same physician: Parth Bhavsar, MD, a board-certified family medicine physician. This MD-only model is designed to provide consistent, high-quality medical care from a physician who knows their patients. No mid-level substitutions.

items.question: Do you see children or only adults?
items.answer: TeleDirectMD sees adults age 18 and older only — pediatric care is not offered. For children, it is recommended to seek an appropriate pediatric provider.

items.question: What makes TeleDirectMD different from other telemedicine platforms?
items.answer: TeleDirectMD is built around direct, one-to-one care from a single board-certified MD — not call-center style routing. Every visit is with the same board-certified family medicine physician. Flat-fee pricing with no subscriptions and no surprise facility charges. Evidence-based, adult-focused virtual urgent care and refills. HIPAA-compliant platform and LegitScript-certified online healthcare operation.

items.question: Why is it called "TeleDirectMD"?
items.answer: The name reflects the design of the service: TeleDirectMD is about restoring a direct, convenient relationship between patient and physician using telemedicine technology.

sectionDescription: Where TeleDirectMD can see you, how location works legally, and what happens if you are traveling.

items.question: Where is TeleDirectMD available?
items.answer: TeleDirectMD is available in more than 35 U.S. states. You must be physically located in one of these states at the time of your visit. The practice is actively expanding and adding new states. For the most up-to-date list, visit the States We Serve page.

items.question: Do I need to live in those states to use TeleDirectMD?
items.answer: No — your permanent home address does not determine eligibility. Telemedicine laws require that you be physically located in a licensed state at the time of your visit. If you are temporarily visiting or traveling through a licensed state, you can still use TeleDirectMD.

items.question: Can I use TeleDirectMD while traveling?
items.answer: Yes — you can use TeleDirectMD while traveling as long as you are physically in one of the states where TeleDirectMD is licensed. If you are outside the licensed states (for example, in a state not covered or in another country), the practice cannot legally provide care at that time.

sectionName: Conditions, Prescriptions, and Scope of Care
sectionDescription: Which problems are appropriate for telemedicine, how prescriptions work, and what falls outside the scope.

items.question: What conditions do you treat?
items.answer: TeleDirectMD treats UTIs, sinus infections, upper respiratory infections, seasonal allergies, pink eye, and more. For a detailed, condition-by-condition list, see the What We Treat page.

items.question: Can you send prescriptions to my local pharmacy?
items.answer: Yes — prescriptions are sent electronically to your preferred pharmacy in the state where you are physically located. During your video visit, the physician confirms your pharmacy information and reviews which medications are safe and appropriate.

items.question: Do you prescribe controlled substances or strong pain medications?
items.answer: No — TeleDirectMD does not prescribe any controlled substances via telehealth. This includes Adderall, benzodiazepines, opioids, and similar medications. The practice does not manage chronic pain or long-term controlled medication regimens.

items.question: Do you prescribe weight-loss medications like GLP-1s (e.g., Wegovy, Ozempic)?
items.answer: TeleDirectMD does not operate a GLP-1 or weight-loss medication program at this time. These medications require a specific program structure. If interested in GLP-1 treatment, it's recommended to discuss options with your in-person primary care provider.

items.question: How do erectile dysfunction (ED) visits and refills work?
items.answer: TeleDirectMD offers ED evaluation and refills within a clearly defined safety framework. Under age 40: Proof of a current ED prescription or prior evaluation is typically required before refilling. Age 40 and above: Treatment may be initiated after a careful history and risk review, if it is safe to do so. Cardiovascular risk factors, medication interactions, and warning symptoms are reviewed. If there are concerns, referral to in-person care is recommended.

items.question: What happens if my condition isn't appropriate for telehealth?
items.answer: If your symptoms require a physical examination, lab work, imaging, or emergency care that cannot be safely managed via telehealth, you will be referred to the appropriate level of care. Patient safety always comes first — if the physician determines that remote treatment is not appropriate, the visit fee situation will be handled accordingly.

sectionDescription: How much visits cost, what is included, and how you can pay (including HSA/FSA cards).

items.question: Do I need insurance to use TeleDirectMD?
items.answer: No — TeleDirectMD is a direct-pay service and does not bill insurance. Visits are charged as a transparent, flat-fee transaction. You can still use your own insurance card at the pharmacy, lab, or imaging center if those providers accept it.

items.question: How much does a visit cost, and are there any hidden fees?
items.answer: A TeleDirectMD visit costs $49, clearly shown at booking — no hidden fees. This covers the physician's time, the video visit, and any prescription sent during the visit. Medication costs at the pharmacy, any labs or imaging, and in-person follow-up visits with other clinicians are not included.

items.question: Can I use HSA/FSA, and what payment methods do you accept?
items.answer: TeleDirectMD accepts major credit and debit cards and many HSA/FSA cards. Payment is processed through a secure platform. If your specific HSA/FSA card does not run directly, you can pay with a regular card and request a receipt/superbill for reimbursement.

items.question: What is your cancellation and refund policy?
items.answer: Whenever possible, please cancel or reschedule ahead of time using the booking system. If your condition is clearly not appropriate for telemedicine and the physician advises immediate in-person care, the fee policy accommodates that situation appropriately.

sectionDescription: How to book, what device you need, whether you need an app, and how your information is protected.

items.question: How do I book an appointment?
items.answer: Booking is straightforward: click the "Book Now" button on the website. After booking, you receive an email and/or text confirmation with a secure video link for your appointment.

items.question: How soon can I be seen?
items.answer: Many patients are seen the same day, often within hours, depending on current schedule availability. If you need a time-sensitive appointment, choose the earliest available slot that fits your schedule.

items.question: How should I prepare for my TeleDirectMD visit?
items.answer: Before your visit, make sure you have: a stable internet connection and a device with a camera and microphone, a quiet and private place where you can speak openly, a list of your current medications, allergies, and medical conditions, and your preferred pharmacy information and any recent blood pressure readings or home test results if relevant.

items.question: Do I need to download an app or create a special account?
items.answer: No app download is required. Your appointment is conducted through a secure, browser-based video link. You simply click the link in your confirmation email or text message at the time of your visit to join.

items.question: Is my visit private and secure?
items.answer: Yes — TeleDirectMD uses a HIPAA-compliant telemedicine platform and a secure electronic health record system. Your health data is used only for your medical care, operations, and as otherwise required by law.

sectionName: Work Notes, Follow-Up, and Portal Access
sectionDescription: How work notes are handled, what forms we do not complete, and how to access your visit summary.

items.question: Can you provide a work or school excuse note?
items.answer: Yes, when medically appropriate — TeleDirectMD can provide a brief work or school excuse note based on the visit. Notes are not backdated and are generally limited to a maximum of three days.

items.question: Do you complete FMLA, disability, long-term accommodation, or DOT/OSHA forms?
items.answer: No — TeleDirectMD does not complete FMLA paperwork, long-term disability forms, detailed workplace accommodation forms, or DOT/OSHA physicals. These typically require in-person examinations and longitudinal follow-up with a primary care physician.

items.question: How do I access my visit summary or instructions after the visit?
items.answer: After your first TeleDirectMD visit, you receive an email invitation to the secure patient portal where you can access your visit summary, instructions, and records.

items.question: How do I contact TeleDirectMD if I have questions after my visit?
items.answer: For non-emergency questions about your visit, prescriptions, or follow-up plan, contact TeleDirectMD through the patient portal or the contact method provided at booking. This contact is for non-emergency issues only and is not a substitute for calling 911 or going to the ER in an emergency.
